meta_title: For Students: deployment workflow to Solve stuck with basic apps (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to deployment workflow and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: For Students: Deployment Workflow to Scale Your AI Apps
content.seo_description: Stop building local-only projects. Master this professional deployment workflow using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your first production-ready AI app.
content.answer_block: You are stuck because local prototypes lack the infrastructure for real-world interaction. To break through, you must transition from local scripts to a CI/CD-driven deployment workflow. By integrating Antigravity for automated environment setup and Claude Code for intelligent CLI orchestration, you bridge the gap between 'code on laptop' and 'live SaaS'.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks perfecting your AI logic, only to watch it wither on your local machine. The 'it works on my computer' trap is the silent killer of student portfolios; it turns potential career-defining projects into abandoned GitHub repositories. You aren't just missing a feature; you're missing the connective tissue that makes software real: a robust deployment pipeline.

Imagine a world where your code travels from your terminal to the cloud with the precision of a seasoned engineer. By adopting a professional deployment workflow, you stop being a student who writes scripts and start being an engineer who ships products. This checklist is your blueprint to move beyond the local environment and launch your first production-ready application.

steps.title: Containerize Your Environment
steps.description: Ensure your app runs identically everywhere by packaging it into a Docker container. This removes environment-specific bugs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a production-ready Dockerfile for a Python/FastAPI AI application. Include multi-stage builds to keep image size small, security best practices for non-root users, and optimized requirements installation.

steps.title: Automate Infrastructure Provisioning
steps.description: Define your cloud infrastructure as code. This allows you to recreate your entire stack in seconds without manual console clicking.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Terraform configuration for an AWS ECS cluster to run a containerized AI app. Include security groups, an application load balancer, and ECR repository setup.

steps.title: Configure CI/CD Pipeline
steps.description: Implement a pipeline that builds, tests, and deploys your code automatically whenever you push to the main branch.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that runs unit tests, builds the Docker image, pushes it to ECR, and triggers a rolling update on ECS.

steps.title: Environment Variable Management
steps.description: Never hardcode API keys. Use a secure secrets manager to inject environment variables at runtime.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Explain how to integrate AWS Secrets Manager with a FastAPI app. Provide a code snippet to fetch secrets at startup without exposing them in logs.

steps.title: Deployment Verification
steps.description: Perform a canary deployment or health check to ensure the new version is stable before routing user traffic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a health check endpoint for a FastAPI app that validates database connectivity and model availability for production monitoring.

pro_tips: Always use a .dockerignore file to prevent local secrets or node_modules from bloating your production images.
Implement health checks that verify the AI model is actually loaded into memory, not just that the server process is alive.
Use 'Infrastructure as Code' (IaC) even for small projects; it builds muscle memory for senior-level systems architecture.
Set up cost-alerting thresholds in your cloud provider immediately to prevent surprise bills from runaway API calls.
Always pin your base image versions (e.g., python:3.11-slim) instead of using :latest to ensure build reproducibility.
content.next_steps: Once your first deployment is live, implement a basic monitoring dashboard using Prometheus or a managed service to track your API latency and error rates.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical roadmap to move from a local hobbyist to a production-grade developer. Don't let your code sit idle—deploy it, share it, and iterate. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
